my_thoughts: 'You should tell me what to do'—demanding I solve their life. Classic passive omnipotence: make me all-powerful so they can be passive. Teacher blocks: 'you'd be following MY decisions.' This shows cost of dependency: lose your own authority. Not 'you should decide for yourself' (prescriptive) but 'here's what happens if I decide' (consequence). Hypothesis: they'll escalate demand or argue they can't decide. Testing if consequence-framing works.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student demands 'You should tell me what to do'—passive omnipotence transferring agency to therapist. Teacher refuses: 'I could, but then you'd be following my decisions not yours.' Blocks dependency by showing consequence. Predicting resist_intellectually because passive-omnipotent patients typically push back when dependency is refused.
